title: Is Planet Labs' Use of NVIDIA's In-Orbit AI Platforms Altering The Investment Case For Planet Labs PBC (PL)?
text:
Planet Labs' implementation of NVIDIA-powered AI directly on its Pelican satellites represents a transformative shift in the satellite imagery business model. Pelican-4 successfully demonstrated in-orbit AI inference powered by NVIDIA's Jetson Orin module, achieving approximately 80% accuracy on object detection tasks. This breakthrough reduces the need for extensive ground processing and enables real-time insights at the edge.

The partnership with NVIDIA accelerates satellite imagery processing from hours to seconds using Blackwell and IGX Thor platforms for ground processing. Additionally, generative AI super-resolution techniques unlock new levels of detail from existing imagery. These technological advances fundamentally alter the investment case for Planet Labs by enabling faster turnaround on customer deliverables and supporting higher-value intelligence products.

Planet Labs' fiscal 2026 results demonstrate the commercial viability of this technology approach. Revenue of $307.7 million (26% growth) and the company's first full year of non-GAAP profitability ($15.5 million adjusted EBITDA) validate the technology strategy. The Defense & Intelligence segment's 50%+ year-over-year growth highlights strong demand for AI-enhanced satellite intelligence from government customers.

The $900 million backlog, with 98% representing recurring annual contract value, suggests customers are willing to pay for these enhanced capabilities. Looking ahead, fiscal 2027 guidance of $415-440 million revenue growth reflects confidence that in-orbit AI processing will drive continued market expansion and support higher margins as the company scales.
